subparts.text: How does negotiation differ from conflict management in resolving workplace issues? 1
What are the key stages of Pondy's Model of Conflict and how do they contribute to the overall process of conflict within an organization? 1
Leadership is a direct function of elements of interpersonal effectiveness. Comment. 2
What are the unwritten and informal rules and regulations which are binding on all members in general? 2
Discuss how the interplay of interpersonal and managerial skills contributes to the success of a manager. 1
What are the main components of active listening? How does it lead to enhanced interpersonal skills. 1

Explain the five conflict management styles in the Thomas- Kilmann model. Provide an example of a situation where a leader might use the 'Competing' style and one where the 'Collaborating' style would be more appropriate. 1

In any organizational setting, groups go through various stages of development. As a leader, your role is critical in guiding the team through these stages. Explain the various stages of group development according to Tuckman’s model and highlight the specific roles a leader during each of these stages to ensure the group's success. 2

Group cohesiveness can reduce tension and provide a supportive environment to the members but the degree of cohesiveness varies from one to another situation. Discuss the main factors responsible for this. 2
Explain the Homans theory of group formation 2
